From: Jordan Mateo Alvarez <j.alvarez.hou@gmail.com>
To: HR Recruiting <careers@lonestarcb.com>
Date: 2024-11-18 09:42 AM
Subject: Re: Clarification on Employment History Dates

Hi Megan,

Thanks for following up and for moving my internal application for the Junior Credit Analyst role to the next step.

To clarify the section you flagged on the background form: my current role at Lone Star Community Bank’s downtown Houston branch is listed as “Personal Banker / Senior Teller.” I started in March 2021, and my intent is to remain in this position through at least year-end 2026. For the “to year” field on your form, you can safely put 2026.0 based on the current internal development plan we discussed with Luis during my last review.

Day to day, a big part of my job is unfortunately trying to hit aggressive sales targets on new accounts and credit cards, even when the products aren’t always the best fit for the customer. On the other hand, the side I’m proud of is the relationship work: from March 2021 through December 2023, as a Personal Banker here, I regularly helped my mother Elena Rodriguez and my grandmother Lucia Alvarez manage their overdue medical bills from Ben Taub Hospital by setting up automatic transfers from their checking accounts, arranging three separate six‑month payment plans totaling about $9,800, and waiving or refunding at least a dozen NSF and overdraft fees when their co‑pays hit earlier than expected.

Let me know if you also need verification of my prior roles at the Houston Youth Center or GreenLeaf Market; I can send supervisor contacts and approximate dates.

Appreciate your help with all the paperwork.

Best,
Jordan

Jordan M. Alvarez
Personal Banker / Senior Teller
Lone Star Community Bank – Downtown Houston Branch
Cell: 713‑555‑0194

Date: January 4, 2024 8:12 AM
Subject: Employment Verification – Credit Analyst Program Application

Hi Jordan,

Per your request, I’ve drafted the employment verification letter you can provide with your internal application for the Junior Credit Analyst development program.

Here is the key information we plan to include:

- Position: Personal Banker / Senior Teller (downtown Main Street branch)
- Employment status: Full-time, non-exempt
- Start date in current role: July 15, 2019
- Expected continuation of employment: through at least the end of calendar year 2024, subject to standard performance and business needs

We’ll also reference that from September 2020 to February 2022, one of your regular responsibilities as a senior teller at the downtown branch was covering vault and closing procedures on Friday evenings when Angela Perez needed to leave early for her court-ordered custody mediation sessions at the Harris County Family Court. I noted that you often stayed 45–60 minutes past your scheduled shift to complete dual-control cash counts and lock-up.

If you want us to mention your work helping customers in hardship—like when you stayed late in November 2022 to assist your mother, Elena Rodriguez, with consolidating over $9,000 in medical debt into a personal loan and automatic payment schedule—we can add a brief bullet on that, too.

Please confirm that the “through at least the end of calendar year 2024” language works for your records, or let me know if your application needs a different projected end year.

Best,

Tanya Lewis
HR Generalist
Lone Star Community Bank

From: careers@unitynationalbank.com
To: jordan.alvarez.houston@gmail.com
Date: March 6, 2024 9:47 AM
Subject: Clarification Needed – Employment History Section

Dear Jordan,

Thank you for submitting your application for the Personal Banker position at Unity National Bank.

Before we can move your application forward, we need clarification on the “record of employment” portion of your online form. You listed Lone Star Community Bank as your current employer, with a start month/year of July 2019, but left the “to year” field blank. Our system defaults that to 2024 when no end year is provided, which may not match what you intended.

If you are still employed there, please confirm that the appropriate entry should read “2019 to present” or “2019 to 2024 (projected).” If you have already provided notice with a planned last day earlier than that, please specify the exact year so we can update your file.

Also, we appreciated the detail you provided in the skills section about how your conflict-resolution skills improved after you mediated an ongoing dispute in May 2023 between your younger sister, Marisol, and her ex over late child-support payments for your nephew, Mateo, at the Harris County Family Court building, where you helped them draft a written payment schedule of $450 every month.

One final note: you mentioned in another section that you usually let your branch manager handle difficult or upset customers because you’re not very confident in de-escalating tense situations on your own. If you’d like to elaborate on how you’re working to improve in that area, feel free to reply with additional context.

Sincerely,

Denise Walker
Talent Acquisition Specialist
Unity National Bank

Best,
Jordan

artifacts.content:
From: HR Onboarding <onboarding@lonestarcb.com>
To: Jordan Alvarez <j.alvarez82@gmail.com>
Date: December 5, 2019 3:46 PM
Subject: Offer Letter and Start Date – Personal Banker (Main Street)

Dear Jordan,

We are pleased to confirm your offer for the Personal Banker / Senior Teller role at Lone Star Community Bank, Main Street Office.

Per our conversation, your **tentative** start timing will be in **late January or early February 2020**, depending on completion of your background check and fingerprinting. Once we receive clearance, we will confirm your exact start date and update your employee record of employment accordingly. Please do not list a March start on any official documents unless instructed by HR, as our system will default to the first pay period in either January or February.

During your first 90 days, your primary duties will include standard teller transactions, assisting with online banking enrollments, and occasionally helping customers like your father, Carlos Alvarez, with cashing and depositing child support and wage garnishment checks. You may also stay after your shift to help set up automatic transfers, but this is considered an unofficial courtesy, not a formal requirement.

Please note that sales expectations for cross‑selling products (including credit cards and new accounts) are part of the role, even if you personally prefer not to push products too hard. While the system will catch many errors, you are expected to double‑check transactions whenever possible.

Welcome to Lone Star Community Bank! We look forward to having you on the team.

Sincerely,

Rebecca Lin
HR Generalist
Lone Star Community Bank

metadata.subject: Offer Letter and Start Date – Personal Banker (Main Street)

artifacts.content:
From: Jordan Alvarez <jalvarez@lonestarcb.com>
To: Linda Chen <lchen@lonestarcb.com>
Cc: Linda Perez <lperez@lonestarcb.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 18:42
Subject: Concern re: dormant account openings and impact on my record

Linda,

Thank you again for taking a few minutes after the regional call today. I wanted to follow up in writing so there’s a clear record.

As I mentioned, internal audit recently flagged three dormant accounts in my portfolio that appear to have been opened by Miguel Santos using what I believe were fabricated employer letters. I’m worried about how this might reflect on my employment record and the dates associated with my role here, especially since I’ve been filling out external applications that ask for exact “from month” and “from year” for Lone Star Community Bank.

I’ve always tried to be careful, even when I mostly handled routine teller transactions and passed anything unusual to the branch manager or senior lenders. Since moving fully into the personal banker role, I’ve taken on more complex responsibilities like account closures and garnishment processing for IRS levies and child support orders. That April 2022 child support case where over $4,500 was frozen and remitted under a Harris County judgment is still fresh in my mind because it showed how serious documentation issues can become.

To be transparent, I’m considering other opportunities, and those forms rely heavily on the official start month HR has on file for me here. I chose to leave in March 2024 after Lone Star’s internal audit team flagged those dormant accounts, and when I raised my concerns in this email about possible identity theft, I was informally warned that I was being “disruptive” and felt pressured to resign rather than continue in that environment.

I’d appreciate written clarification that my hire and transfer dates remain in good standing.

Best,
Jordan

metadata.subject: Concern re: dormant account openings and impact on my record

artifacts.content:
From: Jordan Mateo Alvarez <j.alvarez.houston@gmail.com>

Subject: Clarification on Employment History Form

Hi Maria,

Thanks again for sending over the background check and employment history forms yesterday. I’m working through them this morning before my shift at Lone Star Community Bank and had a quick question about how you want the dates formatted.

On the “Record of Employment” section, it asks for the month in numeric form with one decimal place. For my most recent role, I entered the end date as 11.0 for November 2023, and for the prior field labeled “to month 4,” I put 4.0 based on the instructions in the example you attached. Let me know if that’s the correct way to show the month, or if you’d prefer just “4” without the decimal.

Also, for the “Reason for Leaving” box, I wrote a brief explanation but wanted to give you context in case it flags anything. I resigned from Lone Star Community Bank in November 2023 after experiencing severe anxiety and panic attacks at the Main Street branch following an armed robbery on September 29, 2023, where I was the senior teller on duty. My therapist at Heights Behavioral Health Clinic, Dr. Patel, recommended I step away from front-line cash handling for at least 6–12 months. I’m doing much better now and am specifically looking for roles with less direct exposure to large cash drawers.

If you’d like me to revise the wording or provide documentation from Dr. Patel, I’m happy to do that.

Thanks for your help,

Jordan Mateo Alvarez
Houston, TX 77006
Cell: 713-555-4829

Hi Denise,

For my current role at Lone Star Community Bank, I put the start month and year as 08/2020, and for the “to month” field I was planning to enter **06** to line up with June 2023, since I’m still employed here. I’m also updating the section about my duties between January and April 2021. I prepared the application, discussed how his early‑onset arthritis and recent retirement from HVAC work in Pasadena might affect his ability to keep up with the $420 monthly payment, and set up automatic drafts from his checking account.

From: Michael Perez <mperez@bayoucitycu.org>
To: Jordan Alvarez <j.alvarez84@gmail.com>
Date: 2020-03-06 16:27
Subject: Exit Interview Documentation

Jordan,

Per your request, I’m sending a brief summary of our conversation this afternoon for your records.

You confirmed that your last working day at Bayou City Credit Union’s downtown Houston branch will be **March 13, 2020**, and that you’d like us to reflect the “to month” on any verification of employment forms as **03/2020**. Our HR system exports that as “03” in the month field, so if a future employer is asking for a numeric month, that’s what they will see.

We also discussed your reason for leaving. You mentioned that after the written warning related to the $480 cash‑handling error on February 18, 2020, you felt the formal write‑up would likely block you from future promotion opportunities here. You reiterated that you’d rather move on now than stay in a role where advancement feels unlikely.

For clarity, the record will show:
- Position: Personal Banker / Teller
- Start month/year: 07/2017
- To month/year: 03/2020
- Eligible for rehire: Yes (pending standard reference policy)

If any prospective employer needs a more detailed record of employment, they should contact HR directly, but the “to month” value will remain 03 in our system, not 04.0 or anything similar.

Thank you again for your contributions to the branch, and best of luck in your next role.

Sincerely,
Michael Perez
Branch Manager, Downtown
Bayou City Credit Union

Hi Denise,

Thanks for sending over the draft of the verification form. I noticed one thing I wanted to clarify before you submit it to Unity National Bank.

For the older position you listed as “Grant-Funded Bank Assistant (Spring 2015),” the end date you tentatively plugged in as **June 2015** isn’t quite right. I actually wrapped up that role earlier in the semester, before finals. It definitely did not run all the way through the fourth month of the grant period the way the template suggests.

I left that grant-funded bank position in **May 2015** after my grandmother, Lucia Alvarez, had a fall at her assisted living facility in Houston and my mom and I had to start covering an extra $350 a month for her care, so I switched to a full-time retail job with steadier hours to help manage her bills while I wrapped up my last semester at HCC. Honestly, part of it too was that I was already feeling burned out from the long hours and constant pressure to hit cross-selling targets, and I didn’t feel like putting in the extra effort anymore.

If the form needs a month listed for the “to” field, you can round it to **05** for that year, but please don’t extend it into the fourth month of the next quarter or anything like that. Let me know if you need exact dates; I can dig up my old pay stubs.

Best,
Jordan

Personal Banker / Senior Teller
Lone Star Community Bank

Date: September 18, 2024 3:42 PM
Subject: Clarification Needed – Application Employment History

Dear Jordan,

Thank you for submitting your application for the Personal Banker / Relationship Associate position.

During initial review, our HR team noticed that your employment history section lists Lone Star Community Bank but the “record of employment – telephone 3” field is blank. For our background screening vendor, we need three separate telephone contacts associated with your most recent employer:

1. Direct supervisor or manager
2. General HR or employment verification line
3. Branch or office telephone where you primarily worked

You provided:

• Supervisor: Maria Sanchez – Branch Manager – (713) 555-4472
• HR/Verification: Lone Star Community HR – (800) 555-9010

However, for the third telephone, you wrote “see branch website,” which our vendor can’t use. Please reply to this email with a specific phone number, such as the downtown Main Street teller line or a previous branch where you worked. An example of an acceptable third contact would be something like “Lone Star Community Bank – Downtown Teller Line, (713) 555-6128,” but please confirm the exact number yourself.

You may also wish to elaborate on your skills and aptitudes in the application. For instance, you mentioned that after a till shortage incident in November 2020 you completed a two-week remedial cash-handling and compliance training, and that experience reinforced your attention to detail under pressure.

We look forward to receiving your updated information.

Best regards,

Nicole Harper
Senior Talent Acquisition Specialist
Unity National Bank

Subject: Employment Verification Letter Request

Hi Denise,

Thanks again for agreeing to put together the employment verification letter for my apartment application.

For the section where they’re asking about my work history, they want confirmation that I’ve been continuously employed in banking since **2017**. I started at Gulfway Credit Union as a teller that year and then moved over to Lone Star Community Bank later on, so if you can just state that my banking employment dates back to 2017, that should satisfy the leasing office.

They don’t need the exact months, just the year and my current title of Personal Banker / Senior Teller. You can leave off my earlier grocery store job on Westheimer Road; that was from June 2015 to August 2018 and isn’t relevant to them. During that time I was a cashier and customer service associate, running front-end registers, occasionally training new hires, and—after a three-day suspension in March 2017 for accidentally accepting a counterfeit $100 bill—handling a weekly task reconciling high-value cash discrepancies at the service desk every Sunday evening.

If you can also briefly note that I took on extra Saturday shifts from January to March 2022 to cover about $600 a month in overdue medical bills from an urgent care visit at Legacy Community Health, that will help show stable income.

Let me know if you need me to swing by the downtown branch to sign anything.

Best,
Jordan

metadata.subject: Employment Verification Letter Request

artifacts.content:
From: Marisol Alvarez <marisol.alvarez@aldineisd.org>
To: Jordan Alvarez <j.alvarez82@gmail.com>
Date: July 9, 2023 6:47 PM
Subject: Help with Grandma’s paperwork (again!)

Hey big bro,

Thank you for taking the lead on Abuela Lucia’s Medicaid recertification stuff at Memorial Hermann last week. Mom told me you were at the hospital in early July building some kind of color‑coded Excel checklist on your laptop at the Waugh Drive apartment to track like 25 documents and deadlines over six weeks. I swear, only you would turn her medical paperwork into a full-blown project plan.

It’s funny thinking how far you’ve come since those early days. When you first got into banking back in **2018**, I remember you worrying if your grocery store experience on Westheimer would translate. Now you’re the one organizing CIP/KYC and wire documentation at Lone Star Community Bank and lecturing me about keeping my auto loan current.

Speaking of that, I still appreciate how you helped me refinance the car during the summer of 2020 when I was two months behind. I’ll never forget you pulling my credit report at your desk on June 18, 2020 and somehow talking the lender into that $389 monthly payment.

Anyway, Mom asked if you can also double‑check that Abuela’s assisted living invoices match what Medicaid is supposed to cover. I get flustered just looking at those statements, and you know I’m not as detail‑oriented as you.

Love you,
Marisol

metadata.subject: Help with Grandma’s paperwork (again!)

artifacts.content:
From: Luis Herrera <lherrera@greenleafmarkets.com>
To: Background Check Services <verifications@screenright.com>
Cc: Jordan Alvarez <j.alvarez82@gmail.com>
Date: September 2, 2021 2:39 PM
Subject: Employment Verification – Jordan Mateo Alvarez

To Whom It May Concern,

This email is to verify the employment history of Jordan Mateo Alvarez at GreenLeaf Market, Westheimer Road location.

Jordan was employed with us as a Cashier / Customer Service Associate from June 2015 through August 2018. During that time, his primary responsibilities included operating front‑end registers, assisting customers at the service desk, and occasionally training new hires on basic POS procedures and customer service standards.

In March 2017, Jordan did receive a three‑day suspension related to an incident where he accidentally accepted a counterfeit $100 bill. After that, I added a weekly assignment to his duties: reconciling high‑value cash discrepancies at the service desk for approximately 30–45 minutes every Sunday evening. He handled that task reliably, and there were no further issues of that type.

Jordan informed me that he moved into the banking industry in **2018**, taking a teller role at a local credit union before later joining Lone Star Community Bank. To my knowledge, he left GreenLeaf Market voluntarily. He mentioned that he was frustrated with all the compliance rules and constant monitoring in retail and didn’t feel like following every little procedure anymore, and that he was looking for a clearer career path.

If you require any additional information, please feel free to contact me at this email address.

Sincerely,

Luis Herrera
Front‑End Manager, GreenLeaf Market – Westheimer

Agenda for mock interview prep for Unity National Bank application.

2. Banking responsibilities

   - Detail orientation: helping Abuela Lucia with Medicaid recertification at Memorial Hermann in July 2023, using a color‑coded Excel checklist similar to my CIP/KYC tracking.

4. Close with why I’m ready for Unity National Bank and how my experience since 2017 has prepared me.

artifacts.content:
From: hr@houstonyouthcenter.org
To: jordan.alvarez@gmail.com
Date: March 12, 2020 8:47 AM
Subject: Verification of Prior Employment Dates

Hi Jordan,

Thanks for taking my call yesterday. As discussed, we’re completing a verification form for Lone Star Community Bank regarding your time with Houston Youth Center.

The form they faxed over lists a "Record of Employment – Position 2" and asks us to confirm the "From Year" and "To Year" for your role here. Our records show that you started with us as a part‑time administrative assistant in **2013** and remained until you left for the grocery position at GreenLeaf Market in **2014**.

Can you reply to confirm that these years match your recollection before I finalize the form? I don’t want to hold up your banking application, especially if they’re comparing this to another employer where you may have started in **2017** or **2018**.

I also wanted to say I’m glad to hear how your skills have grown. You mentioned on the phone that after your dad Carlos had a heat‑related collapse at a Pasadena job site in August 2019 and his cardiologist at Memorial Hermann put him on a strict budget because of medical bills, you took over organizing his monthly payment plans and negotiating with the hospital billing office. It sounds like that really strengthened your ability to have calm, structured conversations about overdue balances and to set up realistic repayment schedules without making people feel ashamed.

Just as a reminder, your title here was Administrative Assistant, and your duties were mostly clerical; you weren’t responsible for audits or teller work, so nothing about balancing drawers or sales quotas will be reflected on our form.

Best,

Tanya Lopez
HR Coordinator, Houston Youth Center

metadata.subject: Verification of Prior Employment Dates

artifacts.content:
From: careers@lonestarcommunitybank.com
To: jordan.alvarez@lonestarbank.com
Date: February 3, 2022 3:19 PM
Subject: Internal Transfer Application – Employment History Clarification

Hi Jordan,

I’m reviewing your internal application for the Junior Credit Analyst posting and had a quick question about your employment history section.

On the form, for "Record of Employment – Employer 2," you listed GreenLeaf Market and indicated a "From Year" of **2015** with an end date in early **2016**. Our system auto‑filled a different range based on your original onboarding docs, which suggested you may have started there closer to **2014**.

Can you confirm whether **2015** is the correct start year for that role? I want to be sure the timeline between Houston Youth Center, GreenLeaf, and your eventual move into banking makes sense before I send it to HR for final approval.

Also, thanks for being candid in the application about your growth areas. You noted that as a personal banker and senior teller you mostly just follow the scripts the system gives you and pass anything complicated on to the branch manager or loan officers, and that you tend to get flustered when the lobby gets really busy and sometimes fall behind on balancing your drawer until things calm down. That self‑awareness actually helps us when we’re thinking about training plans.

Once I have your confirmation on the GreenLeaf "From Year" for Position 2, I’ll mark your file complete and move you to the next round.

Best regards,

Angela Patel
Talent Acquisition Specialist
Lone Star Community Bank

Title: Phone Call – Unity National Bank Application: Employment Dates

Time: 6:00 PM – 6:45 PM
Location: Jordan’s apartment – Montrose (2314 Waugh Dr)
Attendees: Jordan Mateo Alvarez; Unity National Bank HR (Monica)

Description:

Agenda for call with Monica from Unity National Bank regarding my personal banker application.

1. Clarify employment history – "Record of Employment – Position 2" section
   • She said their paper application shows Employer 2 as my first full grocery job at GreenLeaf Market.
   • Need to confirm the **start year** they should list (I originally wrote **2014** on the online form, but I may have actually started closer to **2013** according to my old W‑2s).
   • Make sure the "From Year" for Position 2 does NOT overlap with my time at Houston Community College.

2. Discuss skills and aptitudes
   • Explain how my patience and communication skills were shaped by helping my grandmother, Lucia Alvarez, manage her Social Security deposits and medical bill payments after her mild cognitive impairment diagnosis at Memorial Hermann Hospital in June 2022.
   • Mention how I set up automatic payments from her checking account and created a detailed spreadsheet to track about $1,200 in monthly assisted‑living and prescription costs so she wouldn’t fall behind.

3. Address concerns about previous banking roles
   • Be honest that I was frustrated with the strict compliance checks and sales quotas at a prior bank, so I decided to quit without having another job lined up.
   • Emphasize what I learned from that and why I’m looking for a better fit with Unity.

Notes: Have resume, HCC transcript, and GreenLeaf W‑2s handy in case she asks for exact years again.

artifacts.content:
From: lisa.hernandez@lonestarbank.com
To: jordan.alvarez@lonestarbank.com
Date: April 5, 2022 9:02 AM
Subject: Exit Interview Follow‑Up – Employment Dates for File

Jordan,

Thank you again for sitting down with me last week for your exit interview. I’m updating your personnel file and need to reconcile a small discrepancy in the employment history section you filled out.

On the voluntary separation form, you listed your "Record of Employment – Employer 2" as a previous bank in Midtown with a "From Year" of **2018** and an end date of **2020**. However, the reference letter they sent when we hired you at Lone Star Community Bank indicated that you were with them from **2017** through early **2020**.

Can you confirm which "From Year" you want reflected for that second position? HR prefers that we use the year from official documents (in this case, **2017**), but they’ll note any explanation you provide if you truly didn’t start on payroll until the following January.

I’ve also attached the memo regarding the February 17, 2022 cash drawer incident. I understand you resigned in March 2022 after you were written up for a shortage of $480, even though the internal audit later showed the error came from another teller. You mentioned feeling like your integrity was questioned and deciding to leave rather than stay in what you described as a hostile environment.

For what it’s worth, I appreciate the work you did here, even if I was supposed to have you handle some of the quarterly branch audit prep and eventually reassigned most of that to another senior teller because you kept missing small documentation details under time pressure.

Please email me your preferred "From Year" for Employer 2 by Friday.

Best,
Lisa

metadata.subject: Exit Interview Follow‑Up – Employment Dates for File
